name: Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristobal from Calcena — circular by Penas del Cabo
name_original: Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristóbal desde Calcena — circular por Peñas del Cabo
region_intro: Hiking around Calcena offers access to diverse terrain within the mountainous Iberian System, situated in the southern foothills of the Moncayo Natural Park. The region features massifs, plateaus, and valleys, providing varied landscapes for outdoor activities. Hikers will encounter impressive pinnacles and crags, alongside scenic ravines like Barranco de Valdeplata. This area provides a range of hiking trails suitable for different experience levels. Best hiking trails around Calcena The most popular hiking route is Peñas de Herrera - circular from Talamantes, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the surrounding natural landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cueva de los Pilares and Cerro Morrón Loop, a difficult 9.8 miles (15.7 km) path. This trail navigates varied terrain, including ascents to Cerro Morrón. Local hikers also love the Valcongosto Gorges and Cuartún - circular from Purujosa, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through the Valcongosto Gorges, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Calcena is defined by the Moncayo Natural Park, dramatic pinnacles, and deep ravines. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails. The routes in Calcena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Calcena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Calcena?
A: Calcena offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 25 trails available. These routes cater to different fitness levels, ranging from easy strolls to challenging full-day treks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Calcena?
A: The terrain around Calcena is diverse, situated within the mountainous Iberian System and the southern foothills of the Moncayo Natural Park. You can expect massifs, plateaus, and valleys, with impressive pinnacles, crags, and scenic ravines like Barranco de Valdeplata. Some trails involve significant ascents and descents, while others offer more gentle paths.

Q: Are there hiking options for beginners or those looking for an easy walk?
A: Yes, Calcena has options for less experienced hikers. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there is at least one easy trail available. For a moderate option that is not overly strenuous, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971965058/valcongosto-gorges-and-cuartun-circular-from-purujosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorges and Cuartún - circular from Purujosa&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 7.3 km long and takes around 2 hours.

Q: Are there challenging hikes in Calcena for experienced hikers?
A: Absolutely. Calcena offers several difficult routes for those seeking a challenge. One such demanding trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971994351/cueva-de-los-pilares-and-cerro-morron-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares and Cerro Morrón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 16 km and includes significant elevation gain to Cerro Morrón. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971982775/calcena-trasobares-barranco-de-valdeplata-calcena-circular-by-the-moncayo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calcena - Trasobares - Barranco de Valdeplata - Calcena&lt;/a&gt;, a circular route within the Moncayo Natural Park spanning over 26 km.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Calcena?
A: Hikers in Calcena can explore a variety of natural wonders. The region is part of the Moncayo Natural Park, offering spectacular views of the Moncayo mountain. You can encounter impressive geological formations like pinnacles and crags, and scenic ravines such as Barranco de Valdeplata. Notable caves like Cueva Hermosa and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt; are also points of interest, with Cueva Hermosa known for its formations and bat habitat. For panoramic views, consider visiting viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; or the Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristobal.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Calcena?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Calcena are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971963175/penas-de-herrera-circular-from-talamantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de Herrera - circular from Talamantes&lt;/a&gt;, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971994351/cueva-de-los-pilares-and-cerro-morron-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares and Cerro Morrón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971977216/mirador-de-la-ermita-de-san-cristobal-from-calcena-circular-by-penas-del-cabo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristobal from Calcena — circular by Penas del Cabo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Calcena?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Calcena are generally spring, autumn, and summer. These periods offer pleasant weather conditions for exploring the diverse landscapes. While winter can also be suitable, especially for slab climbing on sunny cold days, some higher elevation trails might be affected by snow, particularly within the Moncayo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Calcena?
A: Calcena offers a range of trails, and while specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always explicit, easier and shorter routes would be most suitable. The region's visitor center for the Moncayo Natural Park also provides educational exhibits that can be interesting for families.

Q: Is Calcena a good destination for rock climbing or other outdoor sports?
A: Beyond hiking, Calcena is a recognized spot for rock climbing, boasting hundreds of routes. The Hipocrática via ferrata in Calcena also provides an adventurous way to explore the landscape and enjoy incredible views of the Moncayo Natural Park. The best seasons for climbing are spring, autumn, and summer.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Calcena?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural landscapes within the Moncayo Natural Park, and the variety of trails catering to different experience levels. The dramatic geological formations, such as pinnacles and crags, are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any visitor centers or information points in Calcena?
A: Yes, Calcena hosts a visitor center for the Moncayo Natural Park. This center features an exhibition on cave formation, local flora and fauna, traditional land uses, and an audiovisual presentation about the Sierra del Moncayo, providing valuable information for visitors.
tips:
The Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristóbal is located in the municipality of Los Fayos, in the province of Zaragoza, Aragon. This viewpoint offers spectacular views of the Queiles River Valley and the surrounding area, including the Val reservoir.

The San Cristóbal hermitage, located next to the viewpoint, is a place of great historical and cultural interest. Built in the 17th century, the hermitage is an example of religious architecture in the region. Access to the viewpoint and the hermitage can be made through several hiking routes that run through the area, offering the opportunity to enjoy nature and panoramic views.

The Peñas del Cabo Caves are located on the western cliffs of the Peñas del Cabo, near Calcena, in the province of Zaragoza, Aragon. These caves are known for their impressive geological structure and speleothems, such as stalactites, stalagmites and flowstones.

One of the most popular caves is the Cueva Honda, which has been used as a shelter for livestock and has housed archaeological remains from the Neolithic period. In addition, the cave is an important refuge for bat colonies during the summer.

The caves offer a unique experience for caving and nature lovers.😊
At this point we have a magnificent picture following the GR 90.2 through the Calcena ravine. Nearby we will find the deep cave or the town of Calcena itself
